---
title: Font fallback does not crash LuaLaTeX
# luaotfload appends a ";-fallback" feature to every font name passed to
# add_fallback. A font name terminates only at ":" "(" or "/" in luaotfload's
# request grammar, so a name without one of those swallows the appended
# ";-fallback", the lookup fails, and LuaLaTeX crashes (luaotfload-fallback.lua:
# attempt to index a nil value). Quarto normalizes fallback names in a Lua Meta
# filter: a terminating ":" is appended ONLY when the name has no terminator, so
# documented forms with explicit ":" or options are left untouched (no "::" and
# no trailing ":" after options).
format:
  pdf:
    pdf-engine: lualatex
    keep-tex: true
mainfont: "Latin Modern Roman"
monofont: "Latin Modern Mono"
monofontfallback:
  - "Latin Modern Roman"             # bare name -> ":" appended
  - "Latin Modern Sans:"             # explicit ":" -> unchanged (no "::")
  - "Latin Modern Mono:color=FF0000" # options present -> unchanged (no trailing ":")
_quarto:
  tests:
    pdf:
      ensureLatexFileRegexMatches:
        # The \directlua fallback list is one long line that pandoc may wrap at
        # ~72 columns, so inter-word spaces are matched with \s+ to tolerate an
        # inserted newline.
        -
          - 'luaotfload\.add_fallback\("monofontfallback"'
          - '"Latin\s+Modern\s+Roman:"'    # bare name -> ":" appended
          - '"Latin\s+Modern\s+Sans:"'     # explicit ":" -> unchanged (no "::")
          - '"Latin\s+Modern\s+Mono:color=FF0000"' # options -> unchanged (no trailing ":")
        -
          - '::'
          - 'color=FF0000:'
---

A paragraph with some `inline code` to exercise the monospace font and its
fallbacks.
